title: IEEE and ACM Citation Rules (LLM Audit Rules)
description: Practical checks to validate IEEE and ACM reference/citation conformance: numeric bracket citations, ordering/numbering constraints, DOI/URL expectations, and prose conventions around citations.

sources.source_name: IEEE Editorial Style Manual for Authors
IEEE template instructions on references and citations
ACM Citation Style and Reference Formats

rules.title: Every in-text citation must have a matching reference entry
rules.rationale: Missing bibliography entries break traceability and can be grounds for rejection or major revision.
rules.check: Flag any in-text numeric citation (e.g., [12]) whose number is not present as a leading reference label in the reference list.
rules.pass_criteria: All in-text citation numbers correspond to an entry in the reference list.
exceptions: Draft manuscripts where references are intentionally omitted (must be explicitly indicated).
rules.fix_template: Add the missing reference entry or remove/replace the in-text citation.

rules.title: Every reference entry must be cited in the text
rules.rationale: Uncited references are often disallowed or indicate sloppy citation management.
rules.check: Flag reference-list entries whose index never appears as an in-text citation.
rules.pass_criteria: All reference entries are cited at least once in the body, captions, or appendices as allowed by the venue.
exceptions: Bibliographies used as background reading lists (rare; must be labeled as such).
rules.fix_template: Either cite the reference where relevant or remove it from the reference list.

rules.title: IEEE uses one reference number per distinct reference entry
rules.rationale: IEEE forbids grouping multiple references under one reference number.
rules.check: Flag any single reference number that contains multiple independent sources separated by semicolons or multiple publisher/year patterns.
rules.pass_criteria: Each distinct source has its own reference number entry.
exceptions: One source with multiple parts/chapters is cited as one entry with in-text pinpoint locators.
rules.fix_template: Split the grouped reference into multiple numbered entries and renumber citations accordingly.

rules.title: Do not write 'in reference [n]' mid-sentence; use 'in [n]'
rules.rationale: IEEE editorial guidance prefers concise numeric citation prose.
rules.pass_criteria: Use the form “in [n] …” (and omit author name with the bracket number unless necessary).
exceptions: If the sentence begins with “Reference [n] ...” and cannot be rewritten.
If author name is integral to meaning (rare).
rules.fix_template: Rewrite the clause so it reads “in [n] …” or restructure the sentence to avoid “reference”.

rules.title: Use IEEE cross-reference notation for pinpointing figures/equations/sections
rules.rationale: IEEE specifies bracketed cross-reference notation such as [1, Fig. 2] and [1, eq. (8)].
rules.check: Flag phrases like “Fig. 2 of reference [n]” or “equation (8) of reference [n]”.
rules.pass_criteria: Use IEEE cross-reference notation: [n, Fig. 2], [n, eq. (8)], [n, Sec. IV], etc.
exceptions: Venues within IEEE that explicitly override this detail (rare).
rules.fix_template: Replace with the IEEE bracket cross-reference format, e.g., “in [n, Fig. 2]”.

rules.title: Do not allocate multiple reference numbers to different parts of the same source
rules.rationale: IEEE prefers a single reference entry for a source, with in-text pinpointing for pages/sections/etc.
rules.check: Flag repeated identical author/title/year sources appearing as separate reference-list entries with different numbers.
rules.pass_criteria: One source appears once in the reference list; in-text cites use locators like [n, Ch. 3] as needed.
exceptions: Materially distinct editions or translations; then treat as distinct sources.
rules.fix_template: Merge duplicate entries into one and update in-text citations to pinpoint the relevant location.

rules.title: Reference numbering should match IEEE numeric system conventions
rules.rationale: IEEE systems typically expect stable numeric numbering; renumbering needs consistency.
rules.check: Flag inconsistent or non-numeric reference labels (e.g., [A], [B]) unless the venue profile allows it.
rules.pass_criteria: References are numbered with Arabic numerals in brackets.
rules.fix_template: Convert reference labels to numeric bracketed form consistent across text and reference list.

rules.title: ACM in-text citations are numeric in square brackets
rules.rationale: ACM's reference-formatting guidance specifies numeric bracketed citations (e.g., [1]).
rules.check: Flag non-numeric citation systems (author-year parentheses, footnote-only citations) when ACM profile is selected.
rules.pass_criteria: In-text citations appear as [n] or [n, m] for multiple citations.
exceptions: acmart author-year mode explicitly enabled (then check author-year format instead).
rules.fix_template: Switch to ACM numeric citation mode or enable and consistently use acmart author-year mode.

rules.title: Use ACM Reference Format; nonconforming references may be returned
rules.rationale: ACM explicitly warns that papers may be returned for proper formatting if references do not adhere.
rules.check: Flag mixed reference styles (e.g., APA-like entries) that deviate from ACM examples, especially missing required fields like venue, pages, and DOI when available.
rules.pass_criteria: Reference list matches ACM Reference Format for each entry type.
exceptions: Pre-submission drafts where references are not yet finalized (must be marked).
rules.fix_template: Regenerate the bibliography using ACM-Reference-Format.bst (BibTeX) or the ACM BibLaTeX setup and correct missing fields.

rules.title: Prefer DOIs over URLs when both exist
rules.rationale: ACM guidance prefers DOIs because URLs are not permanent.
rules.check: Flag references that include only a URL when a DOI is known or easily discoverable (common for journal/conference papers).
rules.pass_criteria: If a DOI exists, include it; suppress raw URLs unless a distinct URL is required.
exceptions: No DOI exists (web pages, some technical reports).
A distinct URL is needed in addition to DOI (use distinctURL/archived mechanisms where supported).
rules.fix_template: Add the DOI field; if needed, keep URL with lastaccessed/archived metadata.

rules.title: For web sources, include access date and archival link when feasible
rules.rationale: acmart supports lastaccessed and archived fields to mitigate stale links and content drift.
rules.check: Flag @online or URL-only references without lastaccessed date; flag missing archived links for unstable web content.
rules.pass_criteria: Web resources include lastaccessed and, when available, an archived snapshot link.
exceptions: Publisher-hosted PDFs with long-term stability guarantees (still optional).
rules.fix_template: Add lastaccessed = {Month Day, Year} and archived = {Wayback snapshot URL} when possible.

rules.title: ACM prefers full author names (not initials) in references
rules.rationale: ACM explicitly prefers full names to facilitate indexing and citation linking.
rules.check: Flag references where authors are given only as initials when ACM examples show full names.
rules.pass_criteria: Author names are listed as full given names where available (BibTeX entries should include them).
exceptions: Authors who publish under initials or have unknown given names.
Legacy metadata where full names are unavailable.
rules.fix_template: Replace initials with full given names as in ACM examples or ensure the .bib metadata contains full names.

rules.title: acmart numeric citations are the default; ensure consistency with selected mode
rules.rationale: acmart supports both numeric and author-year; inconsistent use leads to processing delays.
rules.check: Flag mixed citation modes (some [n], some (Author, Year)) in the same manuscript.
rules.pass_criteria: Exactly one citation mode is used consistently across the paper.
exceptions: Explicitly permitted hybrid conventions by a specific ACM venue (rare).
rules.fix_template: Choose either numeric (default) or author-year (\citestyle{acmauthoryear}) and regenerate the bibliography.
